---
title: "Class13"
author: "Yassaman Fakhari (PID A18541145)"
format: pdf
toc: true
---

## Background

Today we're going to do an RNA-seq analysis of a data set on the common glucorticois steroid dexamethasone (dex), and we'll use DESeq for this analysis.

## Data Import 

Let's read the `count` data and `metadata` about this experiment setup from the supplied CVS files:

```{r}
counts<- read.csv("airway_scaledcounts.csv", row.names=1)
metadata<- read.csv("airway_metadata.csv")
```

Have a wee peak:

```{r}
head(counts)
```

And the metadata that tells us what is actually in the columns or our `count` objects:

```{r}
metadata
```

> Q1. How many genes are in this dataset? 

There are `r nrow(counts)` gene in this data set

> Q2. How many ‘control’ cell lines do we have?

```{r}
sum( metadata$dex == "control")
```

```{r}
table(metadata$dex)
```
